title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upserts, Version History, and Tombstones · Retrieval Circuit

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 6167 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove scoped identity, typed upsert outcomes, revisions, tombstones, and replay convergence; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test repeated, reordered, corrected, collided, moved, partial, and retried feed versions converge without duplicate effects.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Calling the same async function twice proves replay safety.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test repeated, reordered, corrected, collided, moved, partial, and retried feed versions converge without duplicate effects. Artifact under review: versioned idempotent entry repository.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Replay Convergence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test repeated, reordered, corrected, collided, moved, partial, and retried feed versions converge without duplicate effects.
